evidence: The value they get in the first six months will be like... yeah. The value they'll have in six years time if they keep up with it will be wild. Those people won't just be users, they'll be like fans and advocates.
Academies have their local towns, regions, catchment areas locked the fuck down. And that is why you can reliably generate talent. If you aren't able to do that, you don't survive as an academy.
The company that 'owns' a university's career pipeline owns 10 years of talent flow from that institution - territorial lock-in creates trust latency advantage
Companies will never invest in true AI for resume scanning. Because why would you process a resource where the ratio is like 99 crap for 1% signal. And even that could be totally fake now.
Ivor suggested Handshake unprompted; V confirmed it's the 'actual perfect acquirer'. Ivor's new Head of Strategic Ops will reach out after joining.
Original hypothesis VALIDATED: conversational modality is the correct way to capture human data

source_display: Quiet Part Problem as Market Opportunity
evidence: Employers have selection criteria they cannot legally state. The gap between stated job requirements and actual selection preferences is a structural feature of hiring markets.
What we are is talent scanning, and that's literally the only thing that matters. - Reframes product priorities, marketing messaging, and technical roadmap.

An Indian recruiter placing US engineers said: 'I have nothing to give these people besides the resume. All of these people look the same.' - Recruiters who survive will need access to information that doesn't exist in public profiles.
If Careerspan's candidates respond at higher rates than other sources, that's sufficient proof of value. Use response rates as the convincing proxy — 'our users pay attention to our recommendations'
Superposition wouldn't bring users. They might not even bring significant revenue. But their logo creates a trust cascade. This is pure signaling economics—the brand IS the product.
V's counter to 'you only have 500 users' was essentially: 'Yes, but they're the right 500.' This inverts the network effects playbook—'we're valuable because we're exclusive.'
Logan's biotech friend disrupted her industry by going directly to morgues instead of through middlemen. 'My middleman is charging me too much money' → go direct to source.
Pursue both brand-name partnerships (Superposition) for credibility AND hungry individual recruiters for execution. Brand names provide website legitimacy.
Prioritize New York City for high-signal user acquisition. Channels: Harvard Club of NYC, Cornell Club of NYC, AI Circle (via Albert), NYC-specific alumni networks.
